text.title: UX Method Index — 32 Methodologies
text.summary: Complete index of 32 UX/UI methodologies mapped to problem type, artifact produced, and required inputs.

list.item: visibility-of-system-status — problem: users unaware of system state; artifact: feedback design spec; input: UI description or prototype
match-real-world — problem: unfamiliar language/conventions; artifact: language and metaphor review; input: UI copy and interaction patterns
user-control-freedom — problem: users trapped in flows without exits; artifact: undo/redo and exit design; input: user flow diagrams
consistency-standards — problem: inconsistent UI behavior; artifact: consistency audit; input: component library and flow screenshots
error-prevention — problem: users make preventable errors; artifact: confirmation and constraint design; input: high-risk action list
recognition-over-recall — problem: users must memorize UI state; artifact: visibility and affordance recommendations; input: task list
flexibility-efficiency — problem: no accelerators for expert users; artifact: shortcut and personalization spec; input: user segmentation data
aesthetic-minimalist — problem: irrelevant information clutters UI; artifact: content priority matrix; input: current UI screens
error-recovery — problem: users cannot recover from errors; artifact: error message copy and recovery flow; input: error taxonomy
help-documentation — problem: users cannot find help; artifact: help architecture and task-focused docs; input: support ticket analysis
user-interviews — question: why? (qualitative motivations and mental models); artifact: interview synthesis, affinity clusters; input: recruited participants, interview guide
usability-testing — question: can users complete tasks? (observable behavior); artifact: usability findings report; input: prototype + recruited participants
surveys — question: how many? (scale, frequency, satisfaction); artifact: quantitative dataset, SUS score; input: survey distribution channel
card-sorting — question: how do users mentally organize content?; artifact: IA recommendations; input: content list + recruited participants
personas — question: who are our users?; artifact: persona canvases; input: actual user interview data or survey responses
journey-mapping — question: what is the end-to-end user experience?; artifact: journey map with emotions and pain points; input: research data or explicit hypothesis label
wireframing — question: what is the layout and content hierarchy?; artifact: low-fidelity wireframes; input: requirements or user stories
prototyping — question: does this interaction work?; artifact: interactive prototype; input: wireframes or design direction
ab-testing — question: which version performs better?; artifact: statistical significance report; input: live traffic, two variants
heuristic-evaluation — question: does this UI violate usability principles?; artifact: heuristic scorecard; input: UI access (screenshots, prototype, or live product)
contextual-inquiry — question: how do users work in their natural environment?; artifact: observation notes, workflow diagrams; input: access to users in their work context
focus-groups — question: what are group attitudes and reactions?; artifact: synthesis report; input: 6-10 recruited participants
tree-testing — question: can users find things in the navigation structure?; artifact: directness score, success rate per task; input: navigation tree + recruited participants
a11y-basics — question: are basic accessibility fundamentals in place?; artifact: quick checklist results; input: UI access
a11y-testing — question: does the product meet WCAG 2.2 AA?; artifact: audit report; input: running product + AT tools
design-critique — question: what can be improved in this design?; artifact: critique notes and action items; input: design artifact + structured critique framework
competitive-analysis — question: what are competitors doing and where are the gaps?; artifact: comparison matrix; input: 3+ direct competitors + 2 adjacent products
content-audit — question: what content exists and what is its quality?; artifact: content inventory spreadsheet; input: live site or content management system access
cognitive-walkthrough — question: can a new user learn this interface without help?; artifact: learnability findings; input: user flow + task list
ia-framework — question: how should content and navigation be structured?; artifact: IA framework document; input: content inventory
ia-templates — question: which IA patterns apply here?; artifact: IA templates and sitemap; input: content types and user goals
mobile-ux-basics — question: does this work on mobile?; artifact: mobile design recommendations; input: mobile design files or prototypes
mobile-ux-patterns — question: which mobile patterns are appropriate?; artifact: pattern selection and specifications; input: use case list
voice-ui-basics — question: how should a voice interface be structured?; artifact: VUI framework and dialogue scripts; input: use case and platform target
voice-ui-patterns — question: which voice interaction patterns apply?; artifact: prompt writing guide and error recovery scripts; input: VUI platform guidelines
diary-study-basics — question: how do user behaviors change over time?; artifact: study plan and entry protocol; input: research question + participant pool
diary-study-execution — question: what longitudinal patterns emerged?; artifact: temporal pattern analysis; input: diary entries over time
